meta_title: For Founders: idea validation to Solve no portfolio (Intermediate Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to idea validation and overcome no portfolio. A Intermediate-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: Idea Validation to Solve No Portfolio
content.seo_description: Stop worrying about your empty portfolio. Learn to validate AI product ideas rapidly using Antigravity and Claude Code to secure your first paying customers.
content.answer_block: Stop chasing portfolio perfection and start solving high-value pain points. Idea validation proves market demand before you write a line of production code. By leveraging Antigravity for rapid prototyping and Claude Code for iterative execution, you transform abstract concepts into revenue-generating assets that serve as your ultimate portfolio.
content.intro:
The cold reality of the SaaS landscape is that clients don't care about your resume; they care about their revenue. You are currently staring at a blank screen, paralyzed by the 'no portfolio' trap—a psychological barrier that keeps you building for yourself rather than for the market.

But here is the secret: your portfolio isn't built on past projects; it's built on your ability to de-risk a business idea in record time. By shifting your focus from 'coding' to 'validating,' you stop being a developer and start being a founder. Let's strip away the fluff and build a validation engine that proves your worth to the market.

steps.title: Define the High-Value Pain Point
steps.description: Identify a specific niche where you can provide 10x value. If you aren't solving a problem that people are already paying to fix, you don't have a product.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a SaaS architect. Analyze current market trends in [Industry] and identify 3 specific, non-obvious pain points that can be solved with a lean AI MVP. Focus on problems with high willingness-to-pay.
steps.claude_code_command: claude research 'market pain points in [Industry] for SaaS'

steps.title: Rapid MVP Architecture
steps.description: Use Antigravity to map out your database schema and API structure. Keep it lean; if it takes more than 48 hours to scaffold, you're over-engineering.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a lean architecture schema for an AI-powered MVP that solves [Pain Point]. Include necessary database tables, API endpoints, and AI integration points using a serverless stack.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate boilerplate for [Stack] based on Antigravity schema'

steps.title: Automated Market Testing
steps.description: Deploy a landing page that captures intent before the product exists. Your goal is to get 50 signups or a waitlist deposit.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a high-converting landing page copy and component structure for a waitlist page. Focus on value-based messaging that targets [Audience] and addresses [Pain Point].
steps.claude_code_command: claude terminal 'deploy landing page to Vercel'

steps.title: Iterative Feature Implementation
steps.description: Use Claude Code to build the core engine. Focus on the 'One Thing' that solves the primary pain point, nothing else.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write the core logic for the [Primary Feature]. Ensure the code is production-ready, modular, and optimized for low-latency AI responses.

steps.title: Validation & Pivot Loop
steps.description: Analyze user feedback from your MVP. If they aren't using it, pivot the messaging or the feature set immediately.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze these user feedback logs: [Logs]. Suggest 3 potential pivots to improve user retention and conversion rates for our AI MVP.
steps.claude_code_command: claude 'refactor code based on user feedback: [Feedback]'

pro_tips: Never build a UI until you have a signed letter of intent or a waitlist of at least 50 qualified leads.
Use Claude Code to write your unit tests first; it forces you to define business logic before implementation.
Your 'portfolio' should be a single case study of how you saved a user X amount of time or money using your tool.
Don't spend more than 3 days on the initial MVP; if it takes longer, you are building a feature, not a business.
Focus on the 'Time-to-Value' metric. If the user doesn't get a result within 60 seconds of signing up, your flow is broken.
content.next_steps: Once your MVP is live and you have your first 10 users, transition from validation to acquisition. Focus on cold outreach and content marketing to drive traffic to your validated solution.
content.conversion_block: Stop waiting for permission to be a founder. The tools exist, the market is waiting, and your first paying customer is one build away. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
